questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correct preposition: 'The ore sample was analysed ____ traces of gold.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Analyse for' is the idiomatic collocation when testing for the presence of something.

**Working.** We test a sample 'for' a substance; the other prepositions do not fit this sense.

**Answer.** for.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A is three times as old as B, and the sum of their ages is $48$ years. A's age is _____ years.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Express both ages in one variable.

**Working.** $A=3B$ and $A+B=48\Rightarrow 4B=48\Rightarrow B=12,\;A=36$.

**Answer.** $36$ years.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: MINER : ORE :: FARMER : ?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The relationship is 'worker : product extracted/produced'.

**Working.** A miner extracts ore; a farmer produces a crop. A tractor and field are tools/locations, not products.

**Answer.** crop.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Problem: frequent equipment breakdowns are delaying production. The most appropriate course of action is to
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A good course of action addresses the root cause.

**Working.** Preventive maintenance reduces breakdowns and therefore the delays.

**Answer.** implement a preventive maintenance schedule.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A man walks $3$ km north and then $4$ km east. His straight-line distance from the starting point is _____ km.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Perpendicular legs form a right triangle; use Pythagoras.

**Working.** $\sqrt{3^2+4^2}=\sqrt{25}=5$.

**Answer.** $5$ km.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Monthly rainfall (in mm) over four months is $10, 20, 30, 40$ (see chart). The total rainfall over the four months is _____ mm.

figure.caption: Bar chart of monthly rainfall: 10, 20, 30, 40 mm.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='220' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='45' y='135' width='28' height='25' fill='#0ea5e9'/><rect x='90' y='110' width='28' height='50' fill='#0ea5e9'/><rect x='135' y='85' width='28' height='75' fill='#0ea5e9'/><rect x='180' y='60' width='28' height='100' fill='#0ea5e9'/><text x='52' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>M1</text><text x='187' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>M4</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total = sum of monthly values.

**Working.** $10+20+30+40 = 100$ mm.

**Answer.** $100$ mm.

questions.subject: RCC & Prestressed Concrete
questions.stem: In a pre-tensioned member, loss of prestress due to elastic shortening is calculated using
options: modular ratio × concrete stress at steel level
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Elastic shortening loss $=m\,f_c$ at the steel level.

**Working.**
$m f_c$.

**Answer.** Modular ratio × concrete stress

questions.subject: Structural Analysis
questions.stem: A fixed beam of span $6\,\text{m}$ carries a central point load $40\,\text{kN}$. The magnitude of the fixed-end moment is _____ kN·m.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Fixed-end moment for a central point load $=\dfrac{WL}{8}$.

**Working.**
$$M=\dfrac{40\times 6}{8}=30.$$

**Answer.** $30\,\text{kN·m}$
